items.title: Scale features before distance
items.avoid: Passing mixed-unit numeric columns directly into k-means.
items.prefer: Apply `StandardScaler` or another documented transform so each feature contributes fairly to Euclidean distance.
items.why: k-means geometry is entirely defined by preprocessed coordinates.

items.title: Treat k as a hypothesis to test
items.avoid: Locking k from one elbow plot or the highest silhouette alone.
items.prefer: Compare 2–3 candidate k values with centroid profiles, stability checks, and stakeholder review.
items.why: Internal metrics optimize mathematical compactness, not business actionability.
items.example: Plot centroids per feature for k=3,4,5; pick the partition whose segments map to known campaign tiers.

items.title: Use robust initialization
items.avoid: Single random centroid seed with `n_init=1` on noisy data.
items.prefer: k-means++ initialization with multiple restarts (`n_init≥10`) and a fixed `random_state` for reproducibility.
items.why: Reduces variance from poor local minima without changing the algorithm's objective.
